subsection.heading: Restraining order; civil penalty
subsection.chapeau: If the Secretary believes that the administration and enforcement of this chapter or an order would be adequately served by such procedure, following an opportunity for an administrative hearing on the record, the Secretary may—

paragraph.content: issue an order to restrain or prevent a person from violating an order; and
assess a civil penalty of not more than $5,000 for violation of such order.

content.p: The district courts of the United States are vested with jurisdiction specifically to enforce, and to prevent and restrain a person from violating, an order or regulation made or issued under this chapter.

subsection.heading: Civil action to be referred to Attorney General

content.p: A civil action authorized to be brought under this section shall be referred to the Attorney General for appropriate action.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1985—Pub. L. 99–198 amended section generally, substituting provisions relating to enforcement for provisions relating to referendum and cattle producer approval of orders, reimbursement of expenses by Secretary, procedural requirements, and bonding requirements.
1978—Pub. L. 95–334 substituted “a majority” for “not less than two-thirds”.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1985 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 99–198 effective Jan. 1, 1986, see section 1601(c) of Pub. L. 99–198, set out as a note under section 2901 of this title.
